file_name: PlayboyEnterprisesInc_20090220_10-QA_EX-10.2_4091580_EX-10.2_Content License Agreement_ Marketing Agreement_ Sales-Purchase Agreement2.txt

perturbation.original_text: Supplier will not sell, ship or otherwise dispose of any such merchandise except upon the order of Purchaser or Playboy; (iii) Supplier will never make, cause others to make or assist others in making, any claim whatsoever to any or all of the Playboy Properties or any trademark, copyright, designation, name, phrase, design or symbol similar thereto in connection with the manufacture, advertising, promotion, sale or distribution of merchandise
perturbation.changed_text: Supplier may sell, ship or otherwise dispose of any such merchandise upon the order of Purchaser. Supplier will make good faith efforts to avoid making claims to the Playboy Properties or any trademark, copyright, designation, name, phrase, design or symbol similar thereto in connection with the manufacture, advertising, promotion, sale or distribution of merchandise;
perturbation.explanation: The original text strictly prohibits the supplier from selling or disposing of merchandise without the explicit order of the Purchaser or Playboy and forbids any claims to Playboy Properties. The modified text introduces flexibility, stating that the supplier *may* sell upon the order of the purchaser and only requires 'good faith efforts' to avoid claims to Playboy Properties. This contradicts the original absolute prohibition, creating ambiguity. This is contradicting against section 5 where the contract shall consist of the entire agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Contract, when attached to a purchase order, shall consist of the entire agreement between the parties and shall supersede any conflicting or contrary terms and conditions of any purchase order or other order form whether supplied by Purchaser or Supplier.
perturbation.justification: The original text strictly prohibited the supplier from selling or disposing of merchandise without explicit order, and from making any claims to Playboy Properties. The modified text permits the supplier to sell upon the purchaser's order and only requires 'good faith efforts' to avoid claims. This creates a direct contradiction with the 'entire agreement' clause, as the original prohibition is no longer absolute. This creates an ambiguity and inconsistencies, as defined above, for whether the original agreement stands in the changed text or not.

perturbation.original_text: Supplier agrees that no production or manufacture of any merchandise covered by this Contract will commence until this Contract has been signed, dated and returned by Supplier to Purchaser. Supplier further agrees that it will not produce, cause to be produced or assist in the production of more units than are specified by Purchaser nor will Supplier produce, cause to be produced or assist in the production of any product or item not specifically requested by Purchaser using any or all of the Playboy Properties or any trademark, copyright, designations, names, phrases, designs or symbols similar to any or all of the Playboy Properties during or at any time after the completion of merchandise requested by this Contract.
perturbation.changed_text: Supplier agrees that production or manufacture of any merchandise covered by this Contract may commence before this Contract has been signed, dated and returned by Supplier to Purchaser if a verbal agreement has been established. Supplier agrees that it may produce, cause to be produced or assist in the production of more units than are specified by Purchaser with a 10% leeway.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states that production cannot start until the contract is signed and returned. The modified text allows production to start based on a verbal agreement, contradicting the written requirement. It also creates a 10% leeway which goes against what the original text specifies. This undermines the entire agreement clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Contract, when attached to a purchase order, shall consist of the entire agreement between the parties and shall supersede any conflicting or contrary terms and conditions of any purchase order or other order form whether supplied by Purchaser or Supplier.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the entire agreement is encompassed in the contract itself, superseding any conflicting terms. The changed text allows for verbal agreements to initiate production, directly contradicting the 'entire agreement' clause. Additionally, introducing a 10% leeway in production quantity clashes with the requirement to adhere to the specific amounts requested by the Purchaser, creating further contradiction and undermining the agreement's written terms. Therefore, this creates a clear and impactful contradiction, as the written terms are no longer the sole determinant of the agreement between the parties as defined in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: This Contract may not be modified or terminated except in writing, and no claimed modification, termination or waiver shall be binding unless also signed by an authorized representative of Playboy.
perturbation.changed_text: This Contract may be modified or terminated either in writing or via email communication from authorized representatives of both parties. A verbal waiver from Playboy representatives is enough to waive.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates that any modification or termination must be in writing and signed by a Playboy representative. The modified text introduces the possibility of modifications via email or even verbal waivers from Playboy representatives. This contradicts the initial requirement for written agreements and introduces uncertainty about the enforceability of contract changes. This directly contradicts paragraph 5, stating that this is the entire agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Contract, when attached to a purchase order, shall consist of the entire agreement between the parties and shall supersede any conflicting or contrary terms and conditions of any purchase order or other order form whether supplied by Purchaser or Supplier.
perturbation.justification: The modified text allows for contract modifications via email or verbal agreements, directly contradicting the original text's requirement for written and signed modifications. This also undermines the contradicted text which states the contract is the entire agreement, thereby introducing ambiguity and a strong in-text contradiction as email or verbal agreements would now be considered valid.
